rule_results.rule: The names on the certificate must match the employee and/or beneficiary names on the form. A maiden name vs. married name difference is acceptable for marriage certificates — if the first names match and the last name difference can be explained by the marriage, treat it as a match.
rule_results.detail: Certificate lists 'Eldon Gormsen' and 'Sara Guy'. Form lists employee 'Eldon Gormsen' and spouse/beneficiary 'Sara Gormsen'. Employee name matches exactly. Spouse first name 'Sara' matches, and last name difference (Guy vs. Gormsen) is explainable as a maiden vs. married name on a marriage certificate.

rule_results.rule: The marriage date must be within the last 12 months of the application date from the form they filled out
rule_results.detail: The date 2026-02-02 is within the last 12 months of 2026-02-02 (cutoff: 2025-02-07)

rule_results.rule: The document must appear to be an official government-issued certificate
rule_results.detail: Attachment text includes formal title 'MARRIAGE CERTIFICATE', jurisdiction 'Any County, Tennessee', reference to 'Marriage Record Book' and page, 'office of the County Clerk', and a '[SEAL]' notation, all consistent with an official government-issued marriage certificate.
